commit.message:
Probabilistic tone: random jacket tier sampling + continuous filler bias

Jacket tone: instead of deterministic tier mapping, sample_tone()
uses Gaussian-weighted random selection centered on the subtitle's
accessibility score. A mainstream subtitle usually gets mainstream
tone but occasionally pop or niche. --tone clamps allowed tiers.

Filler weighting: --tone biases filler selection with a log-space
Gaussian centered on the target frequency range. Replaces rejection
sampling — every draw produces a result, no wasted attempts.
  --tone pop → boosts high-freq fillers (Race, Power, America)
  --tone niche → boosts low-freq fillers (Vasubandhu, Samizdat)
  No --tone → pure sqrt(freq) as before

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(graphs): support passing a graph object directly to GraphExporter (#1032)

## Description

Fixes a logic error in `GraphExporter.__init__` where `GraphCreator` was
unconditionally called regardless of the input type, overwriting any
graph already loaded from a `.pt` file. Extended the fix to handle all
supported input types for the `graph` argument.

## What problem does this change solve?

This is a bugfix. The original code had `GraphCreator(...).create()`
outside the conditional block, meaning it was always executed — even
when a `.pt` file was provided and the graph had already been loaded via
`torch.load`. This silently discarded the loaded graph and attempted to
recreate it from a path that is not a recipe file.

The fix restructures `__init__` to correctly handle all supported input
types:

| Input type | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `HeteroData` | Used directly (no graph creation) |
| `str`/`Path` ending `.pt` | Loaded via `torch.load` |
| `str`/`Path` ending `.yaml`/`.yml` | Built via `GraphCreator` |
| `dict`/`DotDict`/`DictConfig` | Built via `GraphCreator` |

Additional improvements:
- Switched from `str.endswith()` to `Path.suffix` to safely handle both
`str` and `Path` inputs without raising `AttributeError`
- Updated the type annotation of `graph` to `str | Path | HeteroData |
dict | DotDict | DictConfig` to reflect all supported types
- Added imports for `DotDict` (from `anemoi.utils.config`) and
`DictConfig` (from `omegaconf`)
- Updated the error message to mention `.yml` as a supported extension

## What issue or task does this change relate to?

##  Additional notes ##

Minimal single-file change (`graphs/src/anemoi/graphs/export.py`). No
behavioural change for existing `.yaml` string inputs.

commit.message:
Fix strict eval function declaration leak to global scope

Strict-mode eval (both direct and indirect) should scope function
declarations locally — they must not leak to the caller's global
environment. The eval VM shared the same global_this GC pointer as
the caller, so DefineGlobal during eval execution wrote directly to
the shared object. The writeback loop correctly skipped copying to
self.globals, but global_this was already mutated.

Fix: when the writeback loop skips a strict-scoped declaration, also
remove it from global_this to undo the eval VM's direct write.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add mesh topology design: minimum spanning tree, not star

Design for MST-based mesh where each node connects to one neighbor
and routes hop-by-hop via /api/relay. No central hub required.

Key concepts:
- Each node only polls direct neighbors (not all nodes) — critical
  for relay tunnel stability
- Subtree health reporting: neighbors return their peers' status
- Gossip-based routing table from /api/siblings
- Self-healing: disconnected nodes reconnect through any known peer
- Assimilate --join-via: join mesh through any existing member

Most relay infrastructure already exists (/api/relay, /api/siblings,
/api/announce). Main new work: routing table + subtree reporting.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add 5s timeout to clickByElementId to prevent infinite hang

When a clickable element (e.g. gardenTileIcon-2-3) is detected by sight()
but cannot actually be interacted with, page.locator().click() would wait
forever due to ctx.setDefaultTimeout(0). A 5-second timeout causes it to
throw, which browser.ts catches and sends ActionResult.error, allowing
the solver to retry from the next noop.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/nahcnuj/makamujo/sessions/614f6b9b-72c0-44a4-af7c-acaa4cc02740

Co-authored-by: nahcnuj <2093896+nahcnuj@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: skip child elements that inherit cursor:pointer from clickable ancestor

Elements like gardenTileIcon-2-3 and productPrice7 inherit cursor:pointer
from their parent (gardenTile-2-3, product7) without being independent
click targets. sight() was picking them up as false positives, causing the
solver to attempt clicks that would time out.

Now, any element that has an ancestor with an ID and cursor:pointer is
skipped — the ancestor is already in the list as the actual click target.
Also skip elements with pointer-events:none.

- server.ts sight(): cache getComputedStyle(el) once per element and

commit.message:
Add x:Code directive support for inline C# in XAML (#34715)

## Description

Implements the `x:Code` XAML directive, allowing inline C# method
definitions directly in XAML files. This complements the existing XEXPR
(C# Expressions) feature.

Fixes #34712

## What it does

`x:Code` lets you define methods inline in XAML:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage">
    <x:Code><![CDATA[
        void OnButtonClicked(object sender, EventArgs e)
        {
            // inline C# method
        }
    ]]></x:Code>
    <Button Clicked="OnButtonClicked" Text="Click me" />
</ContentPage>
```

## Constraints

- **Requires XSG (XAML Source Generator)** — not supported by Runtime
inflation or XamlC
- **Requires `EnablePreviewFeatures`** — same gate as XEXPR
- **Must be a direct child of the root element** (WPF parity)
- **Must use CDATA** for the code block content
- **Must have `x:Class`** on the root element

## Architecture

x:Code is implemented as a **third source generator pipeline** alongside
CodeBehind (CB) and InitializeComponent (IC):

```
CB → x:Code → IC
```

- `ComputeXCodeSource()` extracts code blocks from parsed XAML
- `XCodeCodeWriter` emits a bare partial class (namespace + class +
verbatim code, no usings)
- The emitted syntax trees feed into the compilation before IC runs
- All IC visitors skip x:Code elements via
`GeneratorHelpers.IsXCodeElement()`

Output hint name format: `{path}_{FileName}.xaml.xcode.cs`

## Diagnostics

| ID | Condition |
|----|-----------|
| MAUIX2012 | `EnablePreviewFeatures` not set (reused from XEXPR) |
| MAUIX2015 | x:Code not a direct child of root element |
| MAUIX2016 | x:Code used without `x:Class` |

## Tests

- **6 SourceGen unit tests** — happy path, multiple blocks, no CDATA,
diagnostics, no-op
- **4 Xaml.UnitTests** — SourceGen success, Runtime failure, XamlC
failure, MockSourceGenerator no-diagnostics

Show existing files (#8697)

## Trello card URL


https://trello.com/c/LuBIi32E/2733-move-attachment-antivirus-scanning-checks-from-google-drive-to-azure

## Changes in this PR:
This PR shows the uploaded files for the uploaded job applications for
both the jobseeker and hiring staff journeys.

It also adds functionality to delete attachments in the:
- religious job application flow to allow jobseekers to delete baptism
certificates
- the uploaded application flow to allow jobseekers to delete uploaded
application forms
- the vacancy creation flow with uploaded application form to allow
hiring staff to delete uploaded their own application forms

This is being done as the first step towards implementing the azure
defender antivirus to replace our existing google drive strategy as,
going forward, we will want users to delete their own files where
possible if they are flagged as malicious and prior to this change users
did not have the ability to delete these uploaded files.

Copilot/update code using design docs (#4)

* Initial plan

* Refactor to new layered architecture

Introduce a layered module structure under lib/src/:

- error/: typed exception hierarchy (FlacMetadataException,
  InvalidFlacException, MalformedMetadataException, etc.) with
  FlacCorruptHeaderException as a backward-compatible alias

- model/: immutable domain model — FlacMetadataBlock abstract base,
  concrete block types (StreamInfoBlock, VorbisCommentBlock, PictureBlock,
  PaddingBlock, ApplicationBlock, SeekTableBlock, CueSheetBlock,
  UnknownBlock), value types (VorbisComments/VorbisCommentEntry,
  PictureType enum, FlacBlockType enum), FlacMetadataDocument

- binary/: byte-level I/O — ByteReader, ByteWriter, FlacParser
  (returns FlacMetadataDocument), FlacSerializer

- edit/: mutation API — sealed MetadataMutation hierarchy,
  FlacMetadataEditor, PaddingStrategy

- transform/: FlacTransformer, FlacTransformPlan/Result/Options

- api/: top-level convenience functions (readFlacMetadata,
  applyMutations, transformFlac)

Remove old flat files (models.dart, flac_parser.dart, flac_editor.dart,
vorbis_comment_processor.dart, picture_block_processor.dart).

Update lib/dart_metaflac.dart to export all new symbols.
Update bin/metaflac.dart and all 4 test files to use the new API.

All 25 tests pass.

commit.message:
fix(pihole): add DNSMASQ_USER=root to prevent FTL capability error

Pi-hole v6 FTL tries to set CAP_SETFCAP before dropping to non-root,
which fails in a Kubernetes security context. Setting DNSMASQ_USER=root
keeps FTL running as root and bypasses the capability elevation.

commit.message:
fix: extract shared parse_token_int and add value-equality tests (#880)

Extract a shared parse_token_int() helper in models.py that centralises
the token-validation rules used by both _sanitize_non_numeric_tokens
(Pydantic boundary) and _extract_output_tokens (parser fast path).
This eliminates the duplicated logic and makes divergence structurally
impossible.

Add TestExtractOutputTokensValueEquality with test_token_value_agreement
parametrized test that verifies numeric counts match (not just the
binary contributes/does-not-contribute verdict) for contributing values.
